Socio-economic history  
Agriculture was the only supporting industry in the past years.
The local socio-economic was remained in the 
traditional self-sufficient agricultural production because of the limitation of farmland area in the mountains.

In  section  (k)  of  Key  Project  Infromation,  the  current  situation  of  socio-economic  for  this  project  is  not  provided clearly.: Socio-economic history   Agriculture was the only supporting industry in the past years. The local socio-economic was remained in the  traditional self-sufficient agricultural production because of the limitation of farmland area in the mountains.  No additional agricultural products could be supplied to the outside. There will be many surplus labours in  slack seasons over a long period. Therefore, many young people had to go to some big cities nearby to make  a living. Low farm income limited the improvement of quality of local farmers’ life. And the descent of forest  soil quality even affected the production of agriculture. Moreover, farmer’s demand for firewood will further  affect natural forest regeneration in winter. The use of fire in agricultural production posed a great threat to  mountains of full miscanthus.   Current situation  China government has issued rural revitalization strategic plan and poverty alleviation policies in recent years.  These policies are aim to support the development of rural area and the local government must reach the  related targets. Public facility is invested by the local government like centralized treatment facilities for rural  sewage and road renovation. These policies can benefit local people and provide the job opportunity. Most  young  men  would  like  to  live  and  work  in  the  big  city,  some  elders  choose  to  go  back  to  look  after  their  relatives.  Agriculture is the traditional way that local people involved in by family unit. They are willing to find  a short-term job to increase family’s incomes.  With the goal to eliminate poverty in all poor counties and  regions by 2020, the villages around this project had been finished the poverty alleviation task. Public services  are  extended  to  these  rural  areas,  for  example,  wastes  collection,  storage  and  transport  service.  All  the  villagers  are  brought  into  rural  medical  insurance  system.  Most  socio-economic  change  is  created  by  government  policies.  This  project  is  also  contributed  to  the  local  socio-economic.  Formal  and  temporary  workers are hired by the project owner, which increases the job opportunities for local people. Furthermore,  workers are more competent through the training provided by the project owner.

In  section  (k)  of  Key  Project  Infromation,  the  current  situation  of  socio-economic  for  this  project  is  not  provided clearly.: Socio-economic history   Agriculture was the only supporting industry in the past years. The local socio-economic was remained in the  traditional self-sufficient agricultural production because of the limitation of farmland area in the mountains.  No additional agricultural products could be supplied to the outside. There will be many surplus labours in  slack seasons over a long period. Therefore, many young people had to go to some big cities nearby to make  a living. Low farm income limited the improvement of quality of local farmers’ life. And the descent of forest  soil quality even affected the production of agriculture. Moreover, farmer’s demand for firewood will further  affect natural forest regeneration in winter. The use of fire in agricultural production posed a great threat to  mountains of full miscanthus.   Current situation  China government has issued rural revitalization strategic plan and poverty alleviation policies in recent years.  These policies are aim to support the development of rural area and the local government must reach the  related targets. Public facility is invested by the local government like centralized treatment facilities for rural  sewage and road renovation. These policies can benefit local people and provide the job opportunity. Most  young  men  would  like  to  live  and  work  in  the  big  city,  some  elders  choose  to  go  back  to  look  after  their  relatives.  Agriculture is the traditional way that local people involved in by family unit. They are willing to find  a short-term job to increase family’s incomes.  With the goal to eliminate poverty in all poor counties and  regions by 2020, the villages around this project had been finished the poverty alleviation task. Public services  are  extended  to  these  rural  areas,  for  example,  wastes  collection,  storage  and  transport  service.  All  the  villagers  are  brought  into  rural  medical  insurance  system.  Most  socio-economic  change  is  created  by  government  policies.  This  project  is  also  contributed  to  the  local  socio-economic.  Formal  and  temporary  workers are hired by the project owner, which increases the job opportunities for local people. Furthermore,  workers are more competent through the training provided by the project owner.

In  section  (k)  of  Key  Project  Infromation,  the  current  situation  of  socio-economic  for  this  project  is  not  provided clearly.: Socio-economic history   Agriculture was the only supporting industry in the past years. The local socio-economic was remained in the  traditional self-sufficient agricultural production because of the limitation of farmland area in the mountains.  No additional agricultural products could be supplied to the outside. There will be many surplus labours in  slack seasons over a long period. Therefore, many young people had to go to some big cities nearby to make  a living. Low farm income limited the improvement of quality of local farmers’ life. And the descent of forest  soil quality even affected the production of agriculture. Moreover, farmer’s demand for firewood will further  affect natural forest regeneration in winter. The use of fire in agricultural production posed a great threat to  mountains of full miscanthus.   Current situation  China government has issued rural revitalization strategic plan and poverty alleviation policies in recent years.  These policies are aim to support the development of rural area and the local government must reach the  related targets. Public facility is invested by the local government like centralized treatment facilities for rural  sewage and road renovation. These policies can benefit local people and provide the job opportunity. Most  young  men  would  like  to  live  and  work  in  the  big  city,  some  elders  choose  to  go  back  to  look  after  their  relatives.  Agriculture is the traditional way that local people involved in by family unit. They are willing to find  a short-term job to increase family’s incomes.  With the goal to eliminate poverty in all poor counties and  regions by 2020, the villages around this project had been finished the poverty alleviation task. Public services  are  extended  to  these  rural  areas,  for  example,  wastes  collection,  storage  and  transport  service.  All  the  villagers  are  brought  into  rural  medical  insurance  system.  Most  socio-economic  change  is  created  by  government  policies.  This  project  is  also  contributed  to  the  local  socio-economic.  Formal  and  temporary  workers are hired by the project owner, which increases the job opportunities for local people. Furthermore,  workers are more competent through the training provided by the project owner.

Section k) of the Key project information document describes the socio-economic situation of the 
communities participating in the project remained in the traditional self-sufficient agricultural 
production because of the limitation of farmland area in the mountains.
No additional agricultural 
products could be supplied to the outside.

By generating jobs and benefits sharing for the local people, the livelihood is improved by the project. None  of the stakeholders expects negative aspects and most of them expect a sustainable development.  To collect  the suggestion and advice, Local stakeholder consultation (LSC) was carried out in Oct 2020. Due to epidemic  prevention,  LSC  was  surveyed  by  questionaries  delivered  to  workers,  local  villagers  and  local  forest  management  officer.  The  questionnaire  covered  contents  of  human  rights,  benefits  and  impacts  to  local  economic, social and lifestyle. Th e feedbacks show this project is benefit to local people.

In  section  (k)  of  Key  Project  Infromation,  the  current  situation  of  socio-economic  for  this  project  is  not  provided clearly.: Socio-economic history   Agriculture was the only supporting industry in the past years. The local socio-economic was remained in the  traditional self-sufficient agricultural production because of the limitation of farmland area in the mountains.  No additional agricultural products could be supplied to the outside. There will be many surplus labours in  slack seasons over a long period. Therefore, many young people had to go to some big cities nearby to make  a living. Low farm income limited the improvement of quality of local farmers’ life. And the descent of forest  soil quality even affected the production of agriculture. Moreover, farmer’s demand for firewood will further  affect natural forest regeneration in winter. The use of fire in agricultural production posed a great threat to  mountains of full miscanthus.   Current situation  China government has issued rural revitalization strategic plan and poverty alleviation policies in recent years.  These policies are aim to support the development of rural area and the local government must reach the  related targets. Public facility is invested by the local government like centralized treatment facilities for rural  sewage and road renovation. These policies can benefit local people and provide the job opportunity. Most  young  men  would  like  to  live  and  work  in  the  big  city,  some  elders  choose  to  go  back  to  look  after  their  relatives.  Agriculture is the traditional way that local people involved in by family unit. They are willing to find  a short-term job to increase family’s incomes.  With the goal to eliminate poverty in all poor counties and  regions by 2020, the villages around this project had been finished the poverty alleviation task. Public services  are  extended  to  these  rural  areas,  for  example,  wastes  collection,  storage  and  transport  service.  All  the  villagers  are  brought  into  rural  medical  insurance  system.  Most  socio-economic  change  is  created  by  government  policies.  This  project  is  also  contributed  to  the  local  socio-economic.  Formal  and  temporary  workers are hired by the project owner, which increases the job opportunities for local people. Furthermore,  workers are more competent through the training provided by the project owner.

However, the document does mention that at least 10.58% of the total project area is being protected from open burning through sustainable land management practices. This is explicitly stated in the context, where it is mentioned that the project areas were designed as buffer zones, accounting for 10.58% of the total project area.

What is the total land area where altern.content: There is no forest management measure applied in the past barren land. When the first tree was planted,  forest conservation had become the most important management task. A number of pruning and thinning  activities will be carried out in high density planting area and replanting measures will be implemented in gap  area to promote and protect the growth of young trees. Pruning will be carried at 4-6 years after planting.  Firewood harvest in  project  area  is  forbidden  when  the  trees  are  young,  and forest  fire  prevention  will  be  strictly enforced. Except then the health and growth of Masson pine or its undergrowth threatens to stagnate  appropriate management will be applied. The long term strategy of turpentine production will start since 15  years after afforestation. Unhealthy trees will be considered to be replaced by some native species such as  broadleaf trees but not to be moved out of the forest for bio-fertilizer use.   Project  Owner  has  prepared  management  procedures  to  cover  the  daily  management.  These  procedures  cover health and safety management, wastes management, fertilize, chemical pesticide and biological control  management.  The  important  guidelines  are  included  in  procedures,  for  example  the  requirement  about  chemical pesticides and biological control agents shall be avoided. Procedures were released in 2014. All the  procedures are kept in the on-site office for rangers to read. There is no forest management measure applied in the past barren land. When the first tree was planted,  forest conservation had become the most important management task. A number of pruning and thinning  activities will be carried out in high density planting area and replanting measures will be implemented in gap  area to promote and protect the growth of young trees. Pruning will be carried at 4-6 years after planting.  Firewood harvest in  project  area  is  forbidden  when  the  trees  are  young,  and forest  fire  prevention  will  be  strictly enforced. Except then the health and growth of Masson pine or its undergrowth threatens to stagnate  appropriate management will be applied. The long term strategy of turpentine production will start since 15  years after afforestation. Unhealthy trees will be considered to be replaced by some native species such as  broadleaf trees but not to be moved out of the forest for bio-fertilizer use.   Project  Owner  has  prepared  management  procedures  to  cover  the  daily  management.  These  procedures  cover health and safety management, wastes management, fertilize, chemical pesticide and biological control  management.  The  important  guidelines  are  included  in  procedures,  for  example  the  requirement  about  chemical pesticides and biological control agents shall be avoided. Procedures were released in 2014. All the  procedures are kept in the on-site office for rangers to read.    These procedures need to be checked and revised to keep consistent with the transition requirements after  this monitoring period.: Other:  /FMM/

4. Quality education.summary: The evidence provided in the Intermediate Answers and Context satisfies the indicator's measurement requirement. The total number of employees who received skill development training is 13 to 14, with 2 females out of a total of 9 new employees. The training services provided include technical and monitoring training, job-specific training, and supervision to safely implement the project. The geographic scope of the skill development training is the local area, with specific locations mentioned including the forest and the workers' hometowns. The evidence directly measures the indicator variable, and therefore, the score is 2.

: Sustainability Monitoring  ID  3  –  Quantitative  employment and  income  generation | As  confirmed  through  checking  the  training  records/TTR/,  it  is  confirmed  that  the  technical  and monitoring  training  have been  provided to all the employees during this monitoring period. Via  interview with the monitoring team/I4/ and forest rangers/I1/, it is  confirmed that trainings have been provided in year 2008, 2013,  2018 and 2020 during this monitoring period which is in line with  the monitoring frequency requested in the plan.   In  conclusion,  the  trainings  were  provided  to  all  the  employees  regularly  during  this  monitoring  period,  this  parameter  can  represent the quality of employment enhanced.  Thus, it is confirmed that the “quality of employment” is positively  improved compared to the baseline.  However, the current situation of parameter for this monitoring  period is not specified.: The chosen parameter is number of jobs created in the operations  of  the  project.  This  parameter  represents  the  quantitative  employment  and  income  generation  due  to  project  operation,  which is monitored once every 20 years. CAR ID  05  Section no.  (n)  Date: 31/12/2020: 1.  This  project  provides  nine  formal  jobs,  including  two  women  employees.  Temporary  jobs  have  been  provided to the local villages when the project needed . Most job opportunities are provided to the villagers  around the project. The income is increasing for them. And most of the workers have not competitiveness  compared to young man. It’s not good choose to work in the bigger city like young man. The project provides  the  opportunity  to  let  them  stay  in  hometown with  their relatives.  Through  the  investigation,  workers  are  satisfied with their jobs.   Fall down in the forest is the most probably health and safety risk. Project owner provide the PPE and safety  training to workers.  And safety procedure is compiled for guiding the daily work. No hurt incident is record in  this monitoring period.  The human and institutional capacity is increased by the socio-economic and ecological benefits of the project,  as the stakeholders agree upon. Local farmers are were trained to become forest range r. Therefore, positive  effects  on  publicity  and  training  can  be  expectedachieved  during  this  monitoring  period.  Fire  prevention  training  was  held  by  project  owner  and  government  every  year.  Other  training,  for  example  forest  management, the carbon sink management requirement, were held from 2008 to 2020 separately.    2. By generating jobs and benefits sharing for the local people, the livelihood is improved by the project. None  of the stakeholders expects negative aspects and most of them expect a sustainable development.  To collect  the suggestion and advice, Local stakeholder consultation (LSC) was carried out in Oct 2020. Due to epidemic  prevention,  LSC  was  surveyed  by  questionaries  delivered  to  workers,  local  villagers  and  local  forest  management  officer.  The  questionnaire  covered  contents  of  human  rights,  benefits  and  impacts  to  local  economic, social and lifestyle. Th e feedbacks show this project is benefit to local people.

The total number of persons employed in managerial positions is not explicitly stated, but it is mentioned that there are 9 employees, including 2 women employees. The proportion of females in managerial positions is approximately 22.2%, but this is based on an incomplete count of managerial positions. The evidence does not satisfy the indicator's measurement requirement due to the lack of explicit quantitative data on the total number of managerial positions.

In  section  (k)  of  Key  Project  Infromation,  the  current  situation  of  socio-economic  for  this  project  is  not  provided clearly.: Socio-economic history   Agriculture was the only supporting industry in the past years. The local socio-economic was remained in the  traditional self-sufficient agricultural production because of the limitation of farmland area in the mountains.  No additional agricultural products could be supplied to the outside. There will be many surplus labours in  slack seasons over a long period. Therefore, many young people had to go to some big cities nearby to make  a living. Low farm income limited the improvement of quality of local farmers’ life. And the descent of forest  soil quality even affected the production of agriculture. Moreover, farmer’s demand for firewood will further  affect natural forest regeneration in winter. The use of fire in agricultural production posed a great threat to  mountains of full miscanthus.   Current situation  China government has issued rural revitalization strategic plan and poverty alleviation policies in recent years.  These policies are aim to support the development of rural area and the local government must reach the  related targets. Public facility is invested by the local government like centralized treatment facilities for rural  sewage and road renovation. These policies can benefit local people and provide the job opportunity. Most  young  men  would  like  to  live  and  work  in  the  big  city,  some  elders  choose  to  go  back  to  look  after  their  relatives.  Agriculture is the traditional way that local people involved in by family unit. They are willing to find  a short-term job to increase family’s incomes.  With the goal to eliminate poverty in all poor counties and  regions by 2020, the villages around this project had been finished the poverty alleviation task. Public services  are  extended  to  these  rural  areas,  for  example,  wastes  collection,  storage  and  transport  service.  All  the  villagers  are  brought  into  rural  medical  insurance  system.  Most  socio-economic  change  is  created  by  government  policies.  This  project  is  also  contributed  to  the  local  socio-economic.  Formal  and  temporary  workers are hired by the project owner, which increases the job opportunities for local people. Furthermore,  workers are more competent through the training provided by the project owner.

In  section  (k)  of  Key  Project  Infromation,  the  current  situation  of  socio-economic  for  this  project  is  not  provided clearly.: Socio-economic history   Agriculture was the only supporting industry in the past years. The local socio-economic was remained in the  traditional self-sufficient agricultural production because of the limitation of farmland area in the mountains.  No additional agricultural products could be supplied to the outside. There will be many surplus labours in  slack seasons over a long period. Therefore, many young people had to go to some big cities nearby to make  a living. Low farm income limited the improvement of quality of local farmers’ life. And the descent of forest  soil quality even affected the production of agriculture. Moreover, farmer’s demand for firewood will further  affect natural forest regeneration in winter. The use of fire in agricultural production posed a great threat to  mountains of full miscanthus.   Current situation  China government has issued rural revitalization strategic plan and poverty alleviation policies in recent years.  These policies are aim to support the development of rural area and the local government must reach the  related targets. Public facility is invested by the local government like centralized treatment facilities for rural  sewage and road renovation. These policies can benefit local people and provide the job opportunity. Most  young  men  would  like  to  live  and  work  in  the  big  city,  some  elders  choose  to  go  back  to  look  after  their  relatives.  Agriculture is the traditional way that local people involved in by family unit. They are willing to find  a short-term job to increase family’s incomes.  With the goal to eliminate poverty in all poor counties and  regions by 2020, the villages around this project had been finished the poverty alleviation task. Public services  are  extended  to  these  rural  areas,  for  example,  wastes  collection,  storage  and  transport  service.  All  the  villagers  are  brought  into  rural  medical  insurance  system.  Most  socio-economic  change  is  created  by  government  policies.  This  project  is  also  contributed  to  the  local  socio-economic.  Formal  and  temporary  workers are hired by the project owner, which increases the job opportunities for local people. Furthermore,  workers are more competent through the training provided by the project owner.

In  section  (k)  of  Key  Project  Infromation,  the  current  situation  of  socio-economic  for  this  project  is  not  provided clearly.: Socio-economic history   Agriculture was the only supporting industry in the past years. The local socio-economic was remained in the  traditional self-sufficient agricultural production because of the limitation of farmland area in the mountains.  No additional agricultural products could be supplied to the outside. There will be many surplus labours in  slack seasons over a long period. Therefore, many young people had to go to some big cities nearby to make  a living. Low farm income limited the improvement of quality of local farmers’ life. And the descent of forest  soil quality even affected the production of agriculture. Moreover, farmer’s demand for firewood will further  affect natural forest regeneration in winter. The use of fire in agricultural production posed a great threat to  mountains of full miscanthus.   Current situation  China government has issued rural revitalization strategic plan and poverty alleviation policies in recent years.  These policies are aim to support the development of rural area and the local government must reach the  related targets. Public facility is invested by the local government like centralized treatment facilities for rural  sewage and road renovation. These policies can benefit local people and provide the job opportunity. Most  young  men  would  like  to  live  and  work  in  the  big  city,  some  elders  choose  to  go  back  to  look  after  their  relatives.  Agriculture is the traditional way that local people involved in by family unit. They are willing to find  a short-term job to increase family’s incomes.  With the goal to eliminate poverty in all poor counties and  regions by 2020, the villages around this project had been finished the poverty alleviation task. Public services  are  extended  to  these  rural  areas,  for  example,  wastes  collection,  storage  and  transport  service.  All  the  villagers  are  brought  into  rural  medical  insurance  system.  Most  socio-economic  change  is  created  by  government  policies.  This  project  is  also  contributed  to  the  local  socio-economic.  Formal  and  temporary  workers are hired by the project owner, which increases the job opportunities for local people. Furthermore,  workers are more competent through the training provided by the project owner.

CAR ID  05  Section no.  (n)  Date: 31/12/2020: 1.  This  project  provides  nine  formal  jobs,  including  two  women  employees.  Temporary  jobs  have  been  provided to the local villages when the project needed . Most job opportunities are provided to the villagers  around the project. The income is increasing for them. And most of the workers have not competitiveness  compared to young man. It’s not good choose to work in the bigger city like young man. The project provides  the  opportunity  to  let  them  stay  in  hometown with  their relatives.  Through  the  investigation,  workers  are  satisfied with their jobs.   Fall down in the forest is the most probably health and safety risk. Project owner provide the PPE and safety  training to workers.  And safety procedure is compiled for guiding the daily work. No hurt incident is record in  this monitoring period.  The human and institutional capacity is increased by the socio-economic and ecological benefits of the project,  as the stakeholders agree upon. Local farmers are were trained to become forest range r. Therefore, positive  effects  on  publicity  and  training  can  be  expectedachieved  during  this  monitoring  period.  Fire  prevention  training  was  held  by  project  owner  and  government  every  year.  Other  training,  for  example  forest  management, the carbon sink management requirement, were held from 2008 to 2020 separately.    2. By generating jobs and benefits sharing for the local people, the livelihood is improved by the project. None  of the stakeholders expects negative aspects and most of them expect a sustainable development.  To collect  the suggestion and advice, Local stakeholder consultation (LSC) was carried out in Oct 2020. Due to epidemic  prevention,  LSC  was  surveyed  by  questionaries  delivered  to  workers,  local  villagers  and  local  forest  management  officer.  The  questionnaire  covered  contents  of  human  rights,  benefits  and  impacts  to  local  economic, social and lifestyle. Th e feedbacks show this project is benefit to local people.

6. Clean water and sanitation.summary: The evidence from the project area does not directly measure the water retention capacity. Although the document mentions that the establishment of the forest can reduce water loss and soil erosion (Document: 2022-10-20+FVerR+Guangdong+AR+GS+1st+Performance+Certification+clean.pdf, Page: 31), no quantitative evidence is provided to support the effectiveness of these measures. The project aims to reduce water loss and soil erosion, and the project owner will try to utilize water conservatively (Document: 3.2+-+Template+-+Local+Stakeholder+Consultation.pdf, Page: 21), but this is a qualitative statement. Therefore, the evidence does not satisfy the indicator's measurement requirement.

In  section  (k)  of  Key  Project  Infromation,  the  current  situation  of  socio-economic  for  this  project  is  not  provided clearly.: Socio-economic history   Agriculture was the only supporting industry in the past years. The local socio-economic was remained in the  traditional self-sufficient agricultural production because of the limitation of farmland area in the mountains.  No additional agricultural products could be supplied to the outside. There will be many surplus labours in  slack seasons over a long period. Therefore, many young people had to go to some big cities nearby to make  a living. Low farm income limited the improvement of quality of local farmers’ life. And the descent of forest  soil quality even affected the production of agriculture. Moreover, farmer’s demand for firewood will further  affect natural forest regeneration in winter. The use of fire in agricultural production posed a great threat to  mountains of full miscanthus.   Current situation  China government has issued rural revitalization strategic plan and poverty alleviation policies in recent years.  These policies are aim to support the development of rural area and the local government must reach the  related targets. Public facility is invested by the local government like centralized treatment facilities for rural  sewage and road renovation. These policies can benefit local people and provide the job opportunity. Most  young  men  would  like  to  live  and  work  in  the  big  city,  some  elders  choose  to  go  back  to  look  after  their  relatives.  Agriculture is the traditional way that local people involved in by family unit. They are willing to find  a short-term job to increase family’s incomes.  With the goal to eliminate poverty in all poor counties and  regions by 2020, the villages around this project had been finished the poverty alleviation task. Public services  are  extended  to  these  rural  areas,  for  example,  wastes  collection,  storage  and  transport  service.  All  the  villagers  are  brought  into  rural  medical  insurance  system.  Most  socio-economic  change  is  created  by  government  policies.  This  project  is  also  contributed  to  the  local  socio-economic.  Formal  and  temporary  workers are hired by the project owner, which increases the job opportunities for local people. Furthermore,  workers are more competent through the training provided by the project owner.

Agriculture is the traditional way that local people involved in by family unit. They are willing to find  a short-term job to increase family’s incomes.  With the goal to eliminate poverty in all poor counties and  regions by 2020, the villages around this project had been finished the poverty alleviation task. Public services  are  extended  to  these  rural  areas,  for  example,  wastes  collection,  storage  and  transport  service.  All  the  villagers  are  brought  into  rural  medical  insurance  system.  Most  socio-economic  change  is  created  by  government  policies.  This  project  is  also  contributed  to  the  local  socio-economic.  Formal  and  temporary  workers are hired by the project owner, which increases the job opportunities for local people. Furthermore,  workers are more competent through the training provided by the project owner.

8. Decent work and economic growth.summary: The evidence does not directly measure the indicator variable 'Total number of employees earning above local minimum wage'. The context mentions that the income is increasing for local people, but it does not provide specific information on the local minimum wage or the number of employees earning above it. The total number of employees in the project is between 13 and 14, but this information does not satisfy the measurement requirement of the indicator. The evidence is qualitative only, and therefore, the score is 1.

CAR ID  05  Section no.  (n)  Date: 31/12/2020: 1.  This  project  provides  nine  formal  jobs,  including  two  women  employees.  Temporary  jobs  have  been  provided to the local villages when the project needed . Most job opportunities are provided to the villagers  around the project. The income is increasing for them. And most of the workers have not competitiveness  compared to young man. It’s not good choose to work in the bigger city like young man. The project provides  the  opportunity  to  let  them  stay  in  hometown with  their relatives.  Through  the  investigation,  workers  are  satisfied with their jobs.   Fall down in the forest is the most probably health and safety risk. Project owner provide the PPE and safety  training to workers.  And safety procedure is compiled for guiding the daily work. No hurt incident is record in  this monitoring period.  The human and institutional capacity is increased by the socio-economic and ecological benefits of the project,  as the stakeholders agree upon. Local farmers are were trained to become forest range r. Therefore, positive  effects  on  publicity  and  training  can  be  expectedachieved  during  this  monitoring  period.  Fire  prevention  training  was  held  by  project  owner  and  government  every  year.  Other  training,  for  example  forest  management, the carbon sink management requirement, were held from 2008 to 2020 separately.    2. By generating jobs and benefits sharing for the local people, the livelihood is improved by the project. None  of the stakeholders expects negative aspects and most of them expect a sustainable development.  To collect  the suggestion and advice, Local stakeholder consultation (LSC) was carried out in Oct 2020. Due to epidemic  prevention,  LSC  was  surveyed  by  questionaries  delivered  to  workers,  local  villagers  and  local  forest  management  officer.  The  questionnaire  covered  contents  of  human  rights,  benefits  and  impacts  to  local  economic, social and lifestyle. Th e feedbacks show this project is benefit to local people.

In  section  (k)  of  Key  Project  Infromation,  the  current  situation  of  socio-economic  for  this  project  is  not  provided clearly.: Socio-economic history   Agriculture was the only supporting industry in the past years. The local socio-economic was remained in the  traditional self-sufficient agricultural production because of the limitation of farmland area in the mountains.  No additional agricultural products could be supplied to the outside. There will be many surplus labours in  slack seasons over a long period. Therefore, many young people had to go to some big cities nearby to make  a living. Low farm income limited the improvement of quality of local farmers’ life. And the descent of forest  soil quality even affected the production of agriculture. Moreover, farmer’s demand for firewood will further  affect natural forest regeneration in winter. The use of fire in agricultural production posed a great threat to  mountains of full miscanthus.   Current situation  China government has issued rural revitalization strategic plan and poverty alleviation policies in recent years.  These policies are aim to support the development of rural area and the local government must reach the  related targets. Public facility is invested by the local government like centralized treatment facilities for rural  sewage and road renovation. These policies can benefit local people and provide the job opportunity. Most  young  men  would  like  to  live  and  work  in  the  big  city,  some  elders  choose  to  go  back  to  look  after  their  relatives.  Agriculture is the traditional way that local people involved in by family unit. They are willing to find  a short-term job to increase family’s incomes.  With the goal to eliminate poverty in all poor counties and  regions by 2020, the villages around this project had been finished the poverty alleviation task. Public services  are  extended  to  these  rural  areas,  for  example,  wastes  collection,  storage  and  transport  service.  All  the  villagers  are  brought  into  rural  medical  insurance  system.  Most  socio-economic  change  is  created  by  government  policies.  This  project  is  also  contributed  to  the  local  socio-economic.  Formal  and  temporary  workers are hired by the project owner, which increases the job opportunities for local people. Furthermore,  workers are more competent through the training provided by the project owner.

In  section  (k)  of  Key  Project  Infromation,  the  current  situation  of  socio-economic  for  this  project  is  not  provided clearly.: Socio-economic history   Agriculture was the only supporting industry in the past years. The local socio-economic was remained in the  traditional self-sufficient agricultural production because of the limitation of farmland area in the mountains.  No additional agricultural products could be supplied to the outside. There will be many surplus labours in  slack seasons over a long period. Therefore, many young people had to go to some big cities nearby to make  a living. Low farm income limited the improvement of quality of local farmers’ life. And the descent of forest  soil quality even affected the production of agriculture. Moreover, farmer’s demand for firewood will further  affect natural forest regeneration in winter. The use of fire in agricultural production posed a great threat to  mountains of full miscanthus.   Current situation  China government has issued rural revitalization strategic plan and poverty alleviation policies in recent years.  These policies are aim to support the development of rural area and the local government must reach the  related targets. Public facility is invested by the local government like centralized treatment facilities for rural  sewage and road renovation. These policies can benefit local people and provide the job opportunity. Most  young  men  would  like  to  live  and  work  in  the  big  city,  some  elders  choose  to  go  back  to  look  after  their  relatives.  Agriculture is the traditional way that local people involved in by family unit. They are willing to find  a short-term job to increase family’s incomes.  With the goal to eliminate poverty in all poor counties and  regions by 2020, the villages around this project had been finished the poverty alleviation task. Public services  are  extended  to  these  rural  areas,  for  example,  wastes  collection,  storage  and  transport  service.  All  the  villagers  are  brought  into  rural  medical  insurance  system.  Most  socio-economic  change  is  created  by  government  policies.  This  project  is  also  contributed  to  the  local  socio-economic.  Formal  and  temporary  workers are hired by the project owner, which increases the job opportunities for local people. Furthermore,  workers are more competent through the training provided by the project owner.

In  section  (k)  of  Key  Project  Infromation,  the  current  situation  of  socio-economic  for  this  project  is  not  provided clearly.: Socio-economic history   Agriculture was the only supporting industry in the past years. The local socio-economic was remained in the  traditional self-sufficient agricultural production because of the limitation of farmland area in the mountains.  No additional agricultural products could be supplied to the outside. There will be many surplus labours in  slack seasons over a long period. Therefore, many young people had to go to some big cities nearby to make  a living. Low farm income limited the improvement of quality of local farmers’ life. And the descent of forest  soil quality even affected the production of agriculture. Moreover, farmer’s demand for firewood will further  affect natural forest regeneration in winter. The use of fire in agricultural production posed a great threat to  mountains of full miscanthus.   Current situation  China government has issued rural revitalization strategic plan and poverty alleviation policies in recent years.  These policies are aim to support the development of rural area and the local government must reach the  related targets. Public facility is invested by the local government like centralized treatment facilities for rural  sewage and road renovation. These policies can benefit local people and provide the job opportunity. Most  young  men  would  like  to  live  and  work  in  the  big  city,  some  elders  choose  to  go  back  to  look  after  their  relatives.  Agriculture is the traditional way that local people involved in by family unit. They are willing to find  a short-term job to increase family’s incomes.  With the goal to eliminate poverty in all poor counties and  regions by 2020, the villages around this project had been finished the poverty alleviation task. Public services  are  extended  to  these  rural  areas,  for  example,  wastes  collection,  storage  and  transport  service.  All  the  villagers  are  brought  into  rural  medical  insurance  system.  Most  socio-economic  change  is  created  by  government  policies.  This  project  is  also  contributed  to  the  local  socio-economic.  Formal  and  temporary  workers are hired by the project owner, which increases the job opportunities for local people. Furthermore,  workers are more competent through the training provided by the project owner.

With the goal to eliminate poverty in all poor counties and  regions by 2020, the villages around this project had been finished the poverty alleviation task. Public services  are  extended  to  these  rural  areas,  for  example,  wastes  collection,  storage  and  transport  service.  All  the  villagers  are  brought  into  rural  medical  insurance  system.  Most  socio-economic  change  is  created  by  government  policies.  This  project  is  also  contributed  to  the  local  socio-economic.  Formal  and  temporary  workers are hired by the project owner, which increases the job opportunities for local people. Furthermore,  workers are more competent through the training provided by the project owner.

Life on land.summary: The evidence from the project report (2022-10-20+FVerR+Guangdong+AR+GS+1st+Performance+Certification+clean.pdf, Page: 38) mentions that the project area of 743.08 hectares has been planted with South China native species, mainly Masson pine, and has reached this size since 2012. However, the total area of the project is 831.01 hectares, and it is not clear what percentage of this area is under sustainable management practices. This evidence is qualitative (Score = 1) as it does not provide explicit quantitative data that directly measures the indicator. The project has implemented specific management practices, including pruning and thinning activities, replanting measures, and firewood harvest prohibition when trees are young, which are mentioned in the project report (2022-10-20+FVerR+Guangdong+AR+GS+1st+Performance+Certification+clean.pdf, Page: 60). The outcomes of these management practices in terms of forest health, biodiversity, and ecosystem services are positive, as mentioned in the project report (2022-10-20+FVerR+Guangdong+AR+GS+1st+Performance+Certification+clean.pdf, Page: 60). However, the area for biological diversity of native ecosystems is unclear, and the method of data collection is unclear (Document: GS+CHINA+PROJECT+REPORT-FINAL.PDF, Page: 15). The key stakeholders involved in the sustainable management of forest areas include local villagers, forest owners, and the government, as mentioned in the project report (2022-10-20+FVerR+Guangdong+AR+GS+1st+Performance+Certification+clean.pdf, Page: 57). The policies, laws, or regulations in place to support and enforce sustainable forest management practices include the policy of China prioritizing sustainable development of the ecological environment, as mentioned in the project report (2022-10-20+FVerR+Guangdong+AR+GS+1st+Performance+Certification+clean.pdf, Page: 57).

The project hired local villagers as rangers. And temporary workers are invited when the need.  More  temporary worker will be hired in turpentine collecting time. The trees are growing up, the forest function is  more and more clear to the air condition, and wild animals.    To fulfill the Gold Standard requirements is also a responsibility of the forest manager, he will do his best and  to make optimization from LSC suggestion.: Documentation provided by project participant (1st round)

Annual Report – year 2017~20194  Conclusion: (a)  a  summary  (in  English  and  local language) of the recent  projects activities | : F In the initial certification/ICR/, it is confirmed that the project  was  implemented  since  2009  by  planting  South  China  native  species  with  mainly  Pinus  massoniana  Lamb.  (Masson  pine)  on  the  degraded  lands  of  831.01  ha  in  Pinglin, Lianshan And Huangdi Village Committee, Youshan  Town, Nanxiong City, Guangdong Province, China.   The size of the project area is 831.01 ha   The size of the planting area is 743.08 ha  The project area was planted by trees during 2009 to 2012  and  has  reached  743.08  hectares  since  2012.  The  intial  project  has  been  registered  as  a  GS  A/R  project  in  2016.  There  are  4  stratifications,  named  under  L1  to  L4.  The  planting and maintenance work were implemented by the  project owner.  For year 2017, via checking the forest management patrol  daily  working  records  for  year  2017/DWR/,  it  is  confirmed  that  the  forest  management,  patrol  and  survey  was  conducted  continuously  in  2017  and  there  are  only  one  species of trees planted in the project area and the trees  growing well. And via interview with the forrest rangers/I1/   and checking the forest management patrol daily working  records for year 2017/DWR/, it is confirmed that there was no  risks including fires, logging or apparent pests or diseases  occurred in year 2017.  or year 2018, via checking the forest management patrol  daily  working  records  for  year  2018/DWR/,  it  is  confirmed  that  the  forest  management,  patrol  and  survey  was  conducted  continuously  in  2018  and  there  are  only  one  species of trees planted in the project area and the trees  growing well. And via interview with the forrest rangers/I1/   and checking the forest management patrol daily working  records for year 2018/DWR/, it is confirmed that there was no  risks including fires, logging or apparent pests or diseases  occurred in year 2018. Hence it is verified that there was no  change since last year. | : OFI 01  OK

There is no forest management measure applied in the past barren land. When the first tree was planted,  forest conservation had become the most important management task. A number of pruning and thinning  activities will be carried out in high density planting area and replanting measures will be implemented in gap  area to promote and protect the growth of young trees. Pruning will be carried at 4-6 years after planting.  Firewood harvest in  project  area  is  forbidden  when  the  trees  are  young,  and forest  fire  prevention  will  be  strictly enforced. Except then the health and growth of Masson pine or its undergrowth threatens to stagnate  appropriate management will be applied. The long term strategy of turpentine production will start since 15  years after afforestation. Unhealthy trees will be considered to be replaced by some native species such as  broadleaf trees but not to be moved out of the forest for bio-fertilizer use.   Project  Owner  has  prepared  management  procedures  to  cover  the  daily  management.  These  procedures  cover health and safety management, wastes management, fertilize, chemical pesticide and biological control  management.  The  important  guidelines  are  included  in  procedures,  for  example  the  requirement  about  chemical pesticides and biological control agents shall be avoided. Procedures were released in 2014. All the  procedures are kept in the on-site office for rangers to read.    These procedures need to be checked and revised to keep consistent with the transition requirements after  this monitoring period.: The updated Key Project Infromation/KPI/ is checked, it is confirmed that forest management applied for this  monitoring period is provided which has been verified by checking the forest management manual/FMM/.

CAR ID  05  Section no.  (n)  Date: 31/12/2020: 1.  This  project  provides  nine  formal  jobs,  including  two  women  employees.  Temporary  jobs  have  been  provided to the local villages when the project needed . Most job opportunities are provided to the villagers  around the project. The income is increasing for them. And most of the workers have not competitiveness  compared to young man. It’s not good choose to work in the bigger city like young man. The project provides  the  opportunity  to  let  them  stay  in  hometown with  their relatives.  Through  the  investigation,  workers  are  satisfied with their jobs.   Fall down in the forest is the most probably health and safety risk. Project owner provide the PPE and safety  training to workers.  And safety procedure is compiled for guiding the daily work. No hurt incident is record in  this monitoring period.  The human and institutional capacity is increased by the socio-economic and ecological benefits of the project,  as the stakeholders agree upon. Local farmers are were trained to become forest range r. Therefore, positive  effects  on  publicity  and  training  can  be  expectedachieved  during  this  monitoring  period.  Fire  prevention  training  was  held  by  project  owner  and  government  every  year.  Other  training,  for  example  forest  management, the carbon sink management requirement, were held from 2008 to 2020 separately.    2. By generating jobs and benefits sharing for the local people, the livelihood is improved by the project. None  of the stakeholders expects negative aspects and most of them expect a sustainable development.  To collect  the suggestion and advice, Local stakeholder consultation (LSC) was carried out in Oct 2020. Due to epidemic  prevention,  LSC  was  surveyed  by  questionaries  delivered  to  workers,  local  villagers  and  local  forest  management  officer.  The  questionnaire  covered  contents  of  human  rights,  benefits  and  impacts  to  local  economic, social and lifestyle. Th e feedbacks show this project is benefit to local people.

The  questionnaire  covered  contents  of  human  rights,  benefits  and  impacts  to  local  economic, social and lifestyle. Th e feedbacks show this project is benefit to local people.

15. Life on land.indicator: Forest areas managed sustainably for forest products including sustainable produced fuelwood

15. Life on land.summary: The provided context does not contain explicit quantitative data to directly measure the total above and below ground biomass stock in forests. The inventory results and growth-model provided are for above-ground biomass, not below-ground biomass, and do not provide a method or value for estimating below-ground biomass. The context mentions the identification of two carbon pools for accounting carbon stock changes, but does not provide a method or value for estimating below-ground biomass. Therefore, the evidence does not satisfy the indicator's measurement requirement.

There is no forest management measure applied in the past barren land. When the first tree was planted,  forest conservation had become the most important management task. A number of pruning and thinning  activities will be carried out in high density planting area and replanting measures will be implemented in gap  area to promote and protect the growth of young trees. Pruning will be carried at 4-6 years after planting.  Firewood harvest in  project  area  is  forbidden  when  the  trees  are  young,  and forest  fire  prevention  will  be  strictly enforced. Except then the health and growth of Masson pine or its undergrowth threatens to stagnate  appropriate management will be applied. The long term strategy of turpentine production will start since 15  years after afforestation. Unhealthy trees will be considered to be replaced by some native species such as  broadleaf trees but not to be moved out of the forest for bio-fertilizer use.   Project  Owner  has  prepared  management  procedures  to  cover  the  daily  management.  These  procedures  cover health and safety management, wastes management, fertilize, chemical pesticide and biological control  management.  The  important  guidelines  are  included  in  procedures,  for  example  the  requirement  about  chemical pesticides and biological control agents shall be avoided. Procedures were released in 2014. All the  procedures are kept in the on-site office for rangers to read.    These procedures need to be checked and revised to keep consistent with the transition requirements after  this monitoring period.: Changes in the template  Section(s): l  New version No.:

15. Life on land.summary: The evidence provided in the Intermediate Answers and Context directly measures the indicator variable 'Total area of Trees Planted'. The total area of land on which trees were planted in a given year is 743.08 hectares, as confirmed by the project owner's report (Document: 2022-10-20+FVerR+Guangdong+AR+GS+1st+Performance+Certification+clean.pdf, Page: 38). This quantitative data directly supports the indicator, and the evidence satisfies the measurement requirement.

(Masson  pine)  on  the  degraded  lands  of  831.01  ha  in  Pinglin, Lianshan And Huangdi Village Committee, Youshan  Town, Nanxiong City, Guangdong Province, China.   The size of the project area is 831.01 ha   The size of the planting area is 743.08 ha  The project area was planted by trees during 2009 to 2012  and  has  reached  743.08  hectares  since  2012.  The  intial  project  has  been  registered  as  a  GS  A/R  project  in  2016.  There  are  4  stratifications,  named  under  L1  to  L4.  The  planting and maintenance work were implemented by the  project owner.  For year 2017, via checking the forest management patrol  daily  working  records  for  year  2017/DWR/,  it  is  confirmed  that  the  forest  management,  patrol  and  survey  was  conducted  continuously  in  2017  and  there  are  only  one  species of trees planted in the project area and the trees  growing well. And via interview with the forrest rangers/I1/   and checking the forest management patrol daily working  records for year 2017/DWR/, it is confirmed that there was no  risks including fires, logging or apparent pests or diseases  occurred in year 2017.  or year 2018, via checking the forest management patrol  daily  working  records  for  year  2018/DWR/,  it  is  confirmed  that  the  forest  management,  patrol  and  survey  was  conducted  continuously  in  2018  and  there  are  only  one  species of trees planted in the project area and the trees  growing well. And via interview with the forrest rangers/I1/   and checking the forest management patrol daily working  records for year 2018/DWR/, it is confirmed that there was no  risks including fires, logging or apparent pests or diseases  occurred in year 2018. There is no forest management measure applied in the past barren land. When the first tree was planted,  forest conservation had become the most important management task. A number of pruning and thinning  activities will be carried out in high density planting area and replanting measures will be implemented in gap  area to promote and protect the growth of young trees. Pruning will be carried at 4-6 years after planting.  Firewood harvest in  project  area  is  forbidden  when  the  trees  are  young,  and forest  fire  prevention  will  be  strictly enforced. Except then the health and growth of Masson pine or its undergrowth threatens to stagnate  appropriate management will be applied. The long term strategy of turpentine production will start since 15  years after afforestation. Unhealthy trees will be considered to be replaced by some native species such as  broadleaf trees but not to be moved out of the forest for bio-fertilizer use.   Project  Owner  has  prepared  management  procedures  to  cover  the  daily  management.  These  procedures  cover health and safety management, wastes management, fertilize, chemical pesticide and biological control  management.  The  important  guidelines  are  included  in  procedures,  for  example  the  requirement  about  chemical pesticides and biological control agents shall be avoided. Procedures were released in 2014. All the  procedures are kept in the on-site office for rangers to read.    These procedures need to be checked and revised to keep consistent with the transition requirements after  this monitoring period.: Other:  /FMM/

There is no forest management measure applied in the past barren land. When the first tree was planted,  forest conservation had become the most important management task. A number of pruning and thinning  activities will be carried out in high density planting area and replanting measures will be implemented in gap  area to promote and protect the growth of young trees. Pruning will be carried at 4-6 years after planting.  Firewood harvest in  project  area  is  forbidden  when  the  trees  are  young,  and forest  fire  prevention  will  be  strictly enforced. Except then the health and growth of Masson pine or its undergrowth threatens to stagnate  appropriate management will be applied. The long term strategy of turpentine production will start since 15  years after afforestation. Unhealthy trees will be considered to be replaced by some native species such as  broadleaf trees but not to be moved out of the forest for bio-fertilizer use.   Project  Owner  has  prepared  management  procedures  to  cover  the  daily  management.  These  procedures  cover health and safety management, wastes management, fertilize, chemical pesticide and biological control  management.  The  important  guidelines  are  included  in  procedures,  for  example  the  requirement  about  chemical pesticides and biological control agents shall be avoided. Procedures were released in 2014. All the  procedures are kept in the on-site office for rangers to read. In  section  (k)  of  Key  Project  Infromation,  the  current  situation  of  socio-economic  for  this  project  is  not  provided clearly.: Socio-economic history   Agriculture was the only supporting industry in the past years. The local socio-economic was remained in the  traditional self-sufficient agricultural production because of the limitation of farmland area in the mountains.  No additional agricultural products could be supplied to the outside. There will be many surplus labours in  slack seasons over a long period. Therefore, many young people had to go to some big cities nearby to make  a living. Low farm income limited the improvement of quality of local farmers’ life. And the descent of forest  soil quality even affected the production of agriculture. Moreover, farmer’s demand for firewood will further  affect natural forest regeneration in winter. The use of fire in agricultural production posed a great threat to  mountains of full miscanthus.   Current situation  China government has issued rural revitalization strategic plan and poverty alleviation policies in recent years.  These policies are aim to support the development of rural area and the local government must reach the  related targets. Public facility is invested by the local government like centralized treatment facilities for rural  sewage and road renovation. These policies can benefit local people and provide the job opportunity. Most  young  men  would  like  to  live  and  work  in  the  big  city,  some  elders  choose  to  go  back  to  look  after  their  relatives.  Agriculture is the traditional way that local people involved in by family unit. They are willing to find  a short-term job to increase family’s incomes.  With the goal to eliminate poverty in all poor counties and  regions by 2020, the villages around this project had been finished the poverty alleviation task. Public services  are  extended  to  these  rural  areas,  for  example,  wastes  collection,  storage  and  transport  service.  All  the  villagers  are  brought  into  rural  medical  insurance  system.  Most  socio-economic  change  is  created  by  government  policies.  This  project  is  also  contributed  to  the  local  socio-economic.  Formal  and  temporary  workers are hired by the project owner, which increases the job opportunities for local people. Furthermore,  workers are more competent through the training provided by the project owner.

15. Life on land.summary: The evidence provided in the Intermediate Answers and Context does not directly measure the total land area under sustainable forest management. The total land area under project management practices is 831.01 ha, but the percentage of this area classified as forest is not explicitly stated. The evidence does not satisfy the indicator's measurement requirement for the percentage of forest area. However, the evidence does provide explicit quantitative data on the total land area under project management practices, which directly measures the indicator variable. Therefore, the score for this indicator is 2.

There is no forest management measure applied in the past barren land. When the first tree was planted,  forest conservation had become the most important management task. A number of pruning and thinning  activities will be carried out in high density planting area and replanting measures will be implemented in gap  area to promote and protect the growth of young trees. Pruning will be carried at 4-6 years after planting.  Firewood harvest in  project  area  is  forbidden  when  the  trees  are  young,  and forest  fire  prevention  will  be  strictly enforced. Except then the health and growth of Masson pine or its undergrowth threatens to stagnate  appropriate management will be applied. The long term strategy of turpentine production will start since 15  years after afforestation. Unhealthy trees will be considered to be replaced by some native species such as  broadleaf trees but not to be moved out of the forest for bio-fertilizer use.   Project  Owner  has  prepared  management  procedures  to  cover  the  daily  management.  These  procedures  cover health and safety management, wastes management, fertilize, chemical pesticide and biological control  management.  The  important  guidelines  are  included  in  procedures,  for  example  the  requirement  about  chemical pesticides and biological control agents shall be avoided. Procedures were released in 2014. All the  procedures are kept in the on-site office for rangers to read.    These procedures need to be checked and revised to keep consistent with the transition requirements after  this monitoring period.: Changes in the template  Section(s): l  New version No.:

There is no forest management measure applied in the past barren land. When the first tree was planted,  forest conservation had become the most important management task. A number of pruning and thinning  activities will be carried out in high density planting area and replanting measures will be implemented in gap  area to promote and protect the growth of young trees. Pruning will be carried at 4-6 years after planting.  Firewood harvest in  project  area  is  forbidden  when  the  trees  are  young,  and forest  fire  prevention  will  be  strictly enforced. Except then the health and growth of Masson pine or its undergrowth threatens to stagnate  appropriate management will be applied. The long term strategy of turpentine production will start since 15  years after afforestation. Unhealthy trees will be considered to be replaced by some native species such as  broadleaf trees but not to be moved out of the forest for bio-fertilizer use.   Project  Owner  has  prepared  management  procedures  to  cover  the  daily  management.  These  procedures  cover health and safety management, wastes management, fertilize, chemical pesticide and biological control  management.  The  important  guidelines  are  included  in  procedures,  for  example  the  requirement  about  chemical pesticides and biological control agents shall be avoided. Procedures were released in 2014. All the  procedures are kept in the on-site office for rangers to read.
